roles.name: Regional Manager
roles.description: World's Best Boss — owns the over-arching solution and keeps morale (and the architecture) intact.

roles.name: Assistant (to the) Regional Manager
roles.description: Schedules every meeting in the conference room, runs the timeline, and holds everyone accountable. Beets optional.

roles.name: Office Administrator
roles.description: Keeper of the supply closet and the permission sets — fields, layouts, and party-planning bylaws.

roles.name: Pretzel Day Engineer
roles.description: Builds the Apex and LWCs that make Pretzel Day actually scale across the org.

roles.name: VP of Northeast Sales
roles.description: Sets standards across the bullpen — the senior practice voice with a corner office.

roles.name: Top Salesman of the Branch
roles.description: Closes the largest accounts with practiced craft and the occasional ill-advised prank.

roles.name: Junior Sales Associate
roles.description: Carries discovery and configuration calls under the Top Salesman's mentorship.

roles.name: IT Guy from Corporate
roles.description: Architect of every system that talks to Dunder Mifflin Infinity — including the printers.

roles.name: Website Architect
roles.description: Lays the API and middleware between Dunder Mifflin Infinity 2.0 and the world.

roles.name: Tech Support Temp
roles.description: Learns the craft of integration — webhooks, batches, and resetting Creed's password again.

roles.name: Quality Assurance Auditor
roles.description: Roams the floor with a clipboard finding bugs before they reach the customer (or HR).

roles.name: Director of First Impressions
roles.description: Owns brand voice and front-of-house messaging for the entire company. Yes, the receptionist runs marketing now.

roles.name: Brand Strategist
roles.description: Plans campaigns — the office-Olympics-grade strategist of conversion and gather.

roles.name: Promotional Materials Lead
roles.description: Carries the campaign into the field — Dundies, brochures, and the daily commute mug.

roles.name: HR Representative
roles.description: Translates between Sales, Accounting, and Corporate — captures requirements without anyone crying. Usually.

roles.name: Senior VP Director of Sales
roles.description: The most senior builder on staff — when Threat Level Midnight ships, he wrote it.

roles.name: Sales Trainee
roles.description: Fresh out of Cornell — eager, untested, and one promotion away from running the place (badly).

_realmNotes: windowOffsetWeeks values are negative so every realm starts at least one month in the past — past weeks get Time Entries (Step 6), future weeks get Schedules. Workstreams are organised as parent scope items (each with 2-3 child sub-scopes) — the wizard inserts parents first, then children with Parent_WorkStream__c set.

realms.projectName: Scranton Management — Branch Leadership CRM Rollout
Scranton Sales Bullpen — Pipeline & Quota Platform

scranton-management.name: Conference Room — Five Minutes!
scranton-management.description: Discovery sign-off — Michael calls everyone in for an unscheduled all-hands.

scranton-management.name: Dundies Awards Ceremony
scranton-management.description: Build kickoff: morale event launches the delivery phase.

scranton-management.name: Performance Review with Toby
scranton-management.description: Mid-project gate: HR-led stewardship review of the build.

scranton-management.name: Schrute Farms Phase Invoice
scranton-management.description: Phase invoice trigger — beets, bears, billing.

scranton-management.name: Casino Night Go-Live
scranton-management.description: Go-Live: the new branch dashboard launches at Casino Night.

scranton-management.name: Manager's Hand-off
scranton-management.description: Hyper-care close: stewardship passed to the next Regional Manager.

scranton-sales-bullpen.name: Sales Kickoff Meeting
scranton-sales-bullpen.description: Project kickoff: the bullpen is briefed on Q-targets.

scranton-sales-bullpen.name: Office Olympics
scranton-sales-bullpen.description: Build phase: morale-building games run alongside the dev sprint.

scranton-sales-bullpen.name: Quarterly Quota Review
scranton-sales-bullpen.description: UAT gate: the pipeline holds up to David Wallace's review.

scranton-sales-bullpen.name: Salesman of the Year Announced
scranton-sales-bullpen.description: Go-Live: pipeline platform live, top performer crowned.

scranton-sales-bullpen.name: Return to the Bullpen
scranton-sales-bullpen.description: Hyper-care close: the team gets back to selling paper.

scranton-sales-bullpen.name: Year-End Commission Run
scranton-sales-bullpen.description: Final invoice: commissions paid, books closed.

scranton-accounting-warehouse.name: The Audit Begins
scranton-accounting-warehouse.description: Discovery: ledger plane online, the auditor arrives.

scranton-accounting-warehouse.name: Inventory Count Day
scranton-accounting-warehouse.description: Build: warehouse inventory and chart-of-accounts deployed.

scranton-accounting-warehouse.name: Oscar's Reconciliation Review
scranton-accounting-warehouse.description: Solution review with the lead accountant himself.

scranton-accounting-warehouse.name: Forklift Certification Day
scranton-accounting-warehouse.description: Go-Live: warehouse ops platform fully operational.

scranton-accounting-warehouse.name: End-of-Quarter Lock
scranton-accounting-warehouse.description: Project close: books closed, dock secured.

corporate-other-branches.name: Board of Directors Kickoff
corporate-other-branches.description: Kickoff council in the New York boardroom.

corporate-other-branches.name: Branch Closing Announcement
corporate-other-branches.description: Discovery: which branches consolidate, which expand.

corporate-other-branches.name: Weave the Infinity Reports
corporate-other-branches.description: Build: dashboards woven across every branch.

corporate-other-branches.name: Sabre Acquisition Review
corporate-other-branches.description: UAT acceptance: corporate signs off on the rollout.

corporate-other-branches.name: Company-wide Go-Live
corporate-other-branches.description: Go-Live + handoff: every branch on Dunder Mifflin Infinity 2.0.

corporate-other-branches.name: Final Recompense from Corporate
corporate-other-branches.description: Final invoice from David Wallace's office.

Michael Scott.notes: Improv class showcase — Sandals resort optional.

Dwight Schrute.notes: Beet harvest at Schrute Farms — agritourism check-in.

Jim Halpert.notes: Athlead trip to Philadelphia — totally legitimate startup business.

Andy Bernard.notes: Cornell a cappella reunion — Here Comes Treble.

Stanley Hudson.notes: Pretzel Day vacation — early Florida retirement scouting.

Phyllis Lapin.notes: Bob Vance, Vance Refrigeration — anniversary getaway.

Erin Hannon.notes: Looking for her birth parents — quick trip out of town.

Angela Martin.notes: Cat show — Princess Lady is competing.

Kevin Malone.notes: Famous Kevin's Famous Chili relaunch — testing the new pot.

Creed Bratton.notes: Off the grid — do not ask, do not call the authorities.

David Wallace.notes: Suck It — pitching the vacuum prototype to investors.
